$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `doc_mount_documents` repo — a
content-text mount-index sibling-DB repo of Phase 2 (a UUID-as-TEXT natural
key, two plain TEXT columns, one REAL-affinity int counter). One committed
file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-doc-mount-documents-fixture.ts + doc-mount-documents-tier2.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness doc_mount_documents tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-mount-index.db (NOT the main
DB). The builder/oracle point SQLITE_MOUNT_INDEX_PATH at the fixture and read
it back through getRawMountIndexDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that
file by path. Mirrors the doc_mount_points / doc_mount_folders machinery.
FRESH-FIXTURE NOTE: v4's getCollection() creates the table from the current
schema (generateDDL) plus a UNIQUE index on fileId on first access. The
fixture therefore has EXACTLY the 7 schema columns, in schema/Zod field order.
ALL FIELDS SET EXPLICITLY on every create (the schema has no .default(...), so
nothing fires regardless) and both sides insert identical bytes.
AFFINITY: plainTextLength is z.number().int().min(0) — MIN-ONLY (no max) → REAL
by v4's mapToSQLiteType (bound f64). The canonical dump collapses an
integer-valued REAL back to a JSON integer, so 0/137/etc. match v4.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.
BANKED across the corpus: plainTextLength both 0 and >0; content + sha256 +
plainTextLength updated together (mirroring a document re-write); a delete.
